item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"displays a warped set of values, regarding humans as raw material for unspecified purposes, and as wholly disposable for the sake of these purposes."

A little hypocritical of the foundation, wouldnt you say? Not me criticizing the article, just thought that was funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, thank ye. Also yeah, this definitely has a bit of Sandman in it. I plan to maybe make a few tales about the King, so you can see more of his dream world there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't even think of any of this stuff. I guess that I had thought that if the dream is enough to jolt you awake, then it makes sense that normally you'd remember it? But when the King does his Thing, it's implied that the victim's own brain blocks off the memory as a self-preservation tactic, to escape the trauma, and to stop the King Kool-Aid-Manning through your skull.

Thank you for thinking about that bit more than I did. Glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One nitpick:

SCP-4116 has an antimemetic effect affecting the recollection of dreams, with all victims only being able to recall the final dream in the sequence.

Not being able to remember dreams, specifically night terrors, isn't anomalous. When we're asleep we're always dreaming, we can only remember dreams during REM sleep. Then again, we go through multiple sleep cycles, so am I being a dum dum and the anomaly antimemetic-ly affects all but one REM sleep?

That aside, this is great. Hard +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Reclassification as Neutralized pending" good lord. The escalation from logs to test to afterwords was great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty excellent! Reminds me of the Sandman graphic novels (the Neil Gaiman ones.) Kind of a mix of Morpheus and The Corinthian.

My only gripe, I think, is that they clearly seem to be somewhat putting together at the end there that 4116 and the murders are related. I feel as if there would be a little bit more on how they are trying to correlate the two. Otherwise, pretty great!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I'm glad you enjoyed it! I removed the footprints part, you were right. It was a bit corny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lots to love here. First sentence of the SCProcedures are a decent hook, and the anomaly itself is well constructed — there's just enough detail to provide the setup, and a good mental image of the area/subject, but it moves at a brisk pace.

Similarly, I enjoyed the brevity of the interviews — each gives us only enough to make implicit the thing's methods and goals, and it's cryptic without coming off as if it is trying to be.

The only real downside to this for me is how clueless the Foundation comes off as at the end. I feel that there's far too many blatant clues pointing towards escape for them to simply neglect and ponder labelling it Neutralized. To this, I would suggest either allowing them to make the insanely obvious connections, or otherwise remove the face-smackingly loud tell of bloodied footprints in lieu of something less Goosebumbs-level spoopy as a hint that it got out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea how you get Jesus from psycho-cannibal-sentient dream-king, but you do you. I do plan to try my hand at a few tales, once I write up some Plottm and a reference for myself. If anybody else wants to do anything with the King too, go for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This guy put a lot of Jesus imagery in my head, for some reason. I’d be interested to see if he appears in any other articles.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how this turned out! Again, it still freaks me the fuck out. I'm still uneasy as fuck after reading this. This is nightmare fuel in a very literal sense.

Take your +1 and wear that with pride. You rock! ^^

All hail the nightmare king!

The anomaly is a clever one, the test logs have a great balance of building tension and providing exposition, and I really enjoyed the king's perspective on the Foundation. Nice work!

item.title: Authy Poist
item.content:encoded:
Meserach is the bomb, two rounds of crit
Thanks to Modern_Erasmus, KrySalems and Rounderhouse too!

This is one that I've been working on for a while. Based off of actual recurring nightmares I had as a kid, following pretty much exactly what I detailed in the article.

I plan to do more with the character and story if this gets a good reception, maybe write up some tales about the King recruiting/kidnapping some other anomalies as his own knights of the round table. What other characters should he encounter?
